lccdd.comment: Number of layers: Either one layer (with staves at two different radii) or two layers (with staves at four different radii). To be chosen with 'SiWr_nLayers' in detector dimensions xml

display.comment: Hide details on weaker systens, otherwise use 'showDaughters="true"'

define.comment: ATLASPix3 properties
Silicon Wrapper module parameters. Use module of two stitched quads (size of ATLASPix3) one next to another for Silicon Wrapper, multiple of these to form a tile/stave. Periphery in z only
Distance between quads in r-phi
Insensitive region between sensors. 0.2 mm
Overlap of quad modules on two sides of tile/stave along tile/stave to cover quad periphery. 0.4 mm of additional overlap
Passive material properties
Use 1.4 mm of carbon fiber support thickness. Together with 50 µm of Silicon this yields 0.499 % X/X0 (1.4 mm/314 mm + 0.05 mm/93.70 mm = 0.00499)
Same flex assumed as in IDEA vertex: 2 layers of 50 µm Aluminum, 2 layers of 150 µm kapton and 100 µm glue
Distance between sensor modules along thickness when having alternating modules in front and in the back
Silicon wrapper barrel parameters
Tolerance for curved objects
Support structure properties
More than twice that of VTXIB holder (4.4 mm)
Thickness of support tube of SiWr barrel. Naively assume that 100% more tube thickness is needed if one has two layers
Stave properties
Overlap of staves in phi to also always detect particles traversing the SiWr with an angle
First layer properties. First layer consists of staves at two positions (r1 and r2).
Same number of modules so that gaps in r-phi in layer 1 can be filled by layer 2
Offset of half a sensor width
Second layer properties (if second layer is enabled). Consists of staves at two possible positions (r3 and r4)
Place outer stave of second layer as far out as possible
Circumference of 2*pi*SiWrB_r4 divided by width of stave, taking into account desired stave overlap. Ceil to get to integer number
Use same number of staves for both stave types of the layer
Offset of quater a sensor width
Offset of half a sensor width

Module spacing to make silicon wrapper barrel as long as possible (respecting max. length of SiWrB_half_length), solving 2.*SiWrB_half_length=SiWrB_nModules1*SiWr_mod_z+(SiWrB_nModules1-1)*SiWrB_module_spacing
Grouping of modules to use the same module ID but additional sensor IDs to be compliant with GlobalTrackerReadoutID definition.
Silicon wrapper barrel support parameters
Place support tube in the middle of the outer stave of the first and the inner stave of the second layer
Silicon wrapper disk parameters

Tile properties. Two different tiles are used. Half-tiles with three dual-modules along the stave and full-tiles with six dual-modules along the stave. The half-tiles are used at low r to more efficiently tile the area, while the full-tiles are used at larger r.
Six sensors are defined within one tile, three within one half-tile
Grouping of modules to use the same module ID but additional sensor IDs to be compliant with GlobalTrackerReadoutID definition.
One or two disks (depending on 'SiWr_nLayers' parameter) make up the silicon wrapper disk system. Each disk provides at least one hit.
Overlap in r between disks to cover gaps in coverage
Keep an opening of at least this size in r for drift chamber (or other tracker) services going out through crack between SiWr barrel and disk

Each disk consists of rings of tiles at different radii. Up to 32 rings are used, but rings that would fall outside the allocated radius ('SiWrD_support_outer_radius') are ignored. A maximal radius of about 3 meters for the base sensor width is supported.

Number of rings that are used to cover the complete disk. Ceil and 'SiWrD_petals_overlap_r' to avoid gaps in coverage. Subtracting 0.2*SiWr_mod_rphi from outer radius to avoid rings outside envelope. Rings are evenly spaced in r.
The first three disks use tiles with less modules
Pitch of the silicon wrapper sensor, used for segmentation

readout.name: SiWrDummyCollection
readout.comment: For support tube, should not have any hits but better safe than sorry

detector.name: SiWrB
detector.comment: Definition of our "stave" object that is used to build the barrel. This one is used for the first layer

stave.name: SiWrBStave_layer0
stave.comment: Two ATLASPix3-sized sensors, but with only one periphery region each.

detector.comment: Definition of our "stave" object that is used to build the barrel. This one is used for the second layer

stave.name: SiWrBStave_layer1
stave.comment: Here, part of the stave holder comes first as the second layer is outside the support tube
Two ATLASPix3-sized sensors, but with only one periphery region each.

detector.comment: Here we construct the barrel. Staves at r1 and r2 from the first layer, staves at r3 and r4 from the second layer
Rest of stave holders (to optimise mother volumes and avoid overlaps of mother volumes)
Silicon wrapper barrel inner support rings

lccdd.comment: Detailed silicon wrapper disks using tiles of ATLASPix3-sized modules

detector.name: SiWrD
detector.comment: Definition of our "stave" object that is used to build the disks.

module.name: SiWrDStave_halfTile
module.comment: Two ATLASPix3-sized sensors, but with only one periphery region each.

module.name: SiWrDStave_fullTile
module.comment: Two ATLASPix3-sized sensors, but with only one periphery region each.

lccdd.comment: Plugin to define the sensitive surfaces of the disks

plugin.name: DD4hep_GenericSurfaceInstallerPlugin
plugin.comment: The better measurement direction should be in u (by convention), which we want to be in phi for the track momentum measurement. In local sensor coordinates, this is in the y direction (along the SiWr tiles)
The worse measurement direction should be in v (by convention), which we want to be in r. In local sensor coordinates, this is in the x direction (across the SiWr tiles)
